mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive Kubernetes distribution that provides a developer-friendly platform for building, deploying, and managing containerized applications. It includes features such as an integrated developer environment, automated installation, and lifecycle management. Key trade-offs include the need for a subscription for enterprise support and the complexity of managing the platform compared to vanilla Kubernetes.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for interacting with the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

```

> This command downloads the latest OpenShift CLI binary.
commands: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

steps.title: Create an OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new OpenShift cluster in the specified region with the desired version and node count.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> Ensure you have the OpenShift Installer binary in your PATH.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl and oc commands to interact with your new OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures additional features such as monitoring and autoscaling for your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

```

> This command applies the default monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> This command will delete the cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade OpenShift Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster and check for any deprecated APIs. After the upgrade, verify the cluster status.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<new-version-image>

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <new-version-image> with the desired version.
> Verify that all nodes are in the 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the kubeconfig is not pointing to the correct cluster or the cluster's certificate is not trusted.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG environment variable is set correctly and the cluster's CA certificate is included.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no nodes available in cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the nodes are not in a ready state or the cluster is not properly initialized.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes using 'oc get nodes' and troubleshoot any issues.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the specified image cannot be pulled from the container registry.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure that the image is accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster by adding more nodes or upgrading existing nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved developer experience.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the updated security policies and migrate any deprecated configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'oc get nodes' showing all nodes in the 'Ready' state, indicating that your OpenShift cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
